label: Schwaan_1_Stroma_Schwan_myelinating
note: Label uses 'Schwaan' spelling matching the atlas annotation exactly.
evidence: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify Schwann cells as part of the stromal compartment in adult human skin. Two Schwann cell subtypes are identified: myelinating (Schwaan_1) and non-myelinating (Schwaan_2). Myelinating Schwann cells are defined by expression of MBP (myelin basic protein), MPZ (myelin protein zero), and PLP1 (proteolipid protein 1). These cells wrap peripheral axons in myelin sheaths, enabling rapid saltatory conduction. S100B is a pan-Schwann cell marker expressed in both subtypes.
